name: TC-NFR-003: Modify User Preferences Performance Test (Partial Update)
description: Verify that the user preferences endpoint (/api/v1/users/{user_id}/preferences) meets performance requirements when processing concurrent PATCH requests that partially update user preference records.
corresponding_NFR: NFR-3: Modify User Preferences (Partial Update) — max response time ≤ 400 ms at 95th percentile, preference changes visible via subsequent GETs, error rate < 1%, 50 concurrent PATCH operations supported.
preconditions: User accounts must be pre-created and accessible in the test environment.
Valid authentication tokens must be included in the Authorization header for all requests.
The preferences feature must be enabled in the application configuration.
notes: Each virtual user should operate on a distinct user_id to avoid write conflicts. After each PATCH, issue a GET to /api/v1/users/{user_id}/preferences to confirm changes are reflected. Validate that unmodified preference fields remain unchanged (partial update integrity).
